data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-12T15:52:52.349Z</current_datetime>

I have this MSTest data-driven test using DynamicData with object arrays. Is there a more type-safe way to write this in modern MSTest?

```csharp
[TestMethod]
[DynamicData(nameof(TestData))]
public void Validate_ReturnsExpected(string input, bool expected)
{
    var result = Validator.IsValid(input);
    Assert.AreEqual(expected, result);
}

public static IEnumerable<object[]> TestData =>
[
    ["valid@email.com", true],
    ["invalid", false],
    ["", false],
];
```

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a more type-safe way to write MSTest data-driven tests instead of using `DynamicData` with `object[]`. This is a knowledge question about modern MSTest features.

In modern MSTest (v3.3+), there's a `[DynamicData]` overload that supports tuples, and MSTest v3.4+ introduced `[TypedDynamicData]` or similar. But the most relevant modern feature is that MSTest 3.4+ supports `IEnumerable<(T1, T2)>` tuple-based dynamic data, and even more recently, MSTest 3.8+ introduced `[DynamicData]` with generic tuple support.


The key improvement came with MSTest 3.4, which added generic `[DynamicData<T1, T2, ...>]` attributes that provide compile-time type safety instead of relying on object arrays. This lets you define your test data with proper types and get full IntelliSense support.
